notes: Quick query evals (1-4): Both with_skill and without_skill pass all 'no session bloat' assertions — the skill does NOT cause unnecessary overhead on simple factual lookups.
Eval 3 (budget buffer): Both configurations fail the '3 or fewer tool calls' assertion — threshold is too tight for a query that reasonably needs to read 2 files + write 2 outputs.

Eval 5 (breakfast research): Both configurations pass all assertions — the skill properly activates for real planning work. With-skill took ~122s vs ~75s without-skill, but produced a persistent research file following the research methodology.
Time overhead: with_skill averages +5.3s on quick queries (25.7s vs 13.7s mean for evals 1-4) — most of this is reading the SKILL.md itself.
Token overhead: with_skill uses ~7.5K more tokens on average across quick queries — again primarily from loading the skill.
The skill is NOT causing the problematic behavior we feared (full session start protocol on every query). It correctly handles quick lookups with minimal overhead.
